summary: Matched a large portion of Kirby copied-neutral-special code in `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`, primarily filling previously placeholdered Ness, Donkey Kong, and `Pr` special-N routines. The decomp.dev bot reported 42 new matches and +19,284 matched bytes, with the unit `main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs` gaining many 100% functions. Supporting changes corrected Kirby fighter var layout by replacing three adjacent floats with a `Vec3 x8C`, updated initialization to write `.x/.y/.z`, and fixed the public prototype for `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C` to take a `bool` argument.
what_changed: Replaced many `/// #...` placeholders in `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` with matched C implementations, including Ness air neutral-special start/hold animation logic, Donkey Kong neutral-special entry helpers, and many `Pr` special-N animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks.

Added local static inline helpers for recurring matching-sensitive behavior: fake `HSD_JObj` scale/rotation wrappers, scale animation stepping, Ness flash item spawning, `Pr` scale restoration, callback setup, roll SFX playback, hit-capsule toggling, facing direction, and wall-bounce effect handling.

Changed `struct ftKb_FighterVars` layout from separate floats `x8C`, `x90`, `x94` to one `Vec3 x8C`, then updated `ftKb_SpecialN_800F190C` initialization to assign `x8C.x`, `x8C.y`, and `x8C.z` to `1.0F`.

Updated `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C` declarations in `ftKb_Init.h` and `ftKb_Init.dox` from `void ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` to `void ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C(Fighter_GObj* gobj, bool arg1)`.

Implemented `fn_80100E0C` as a damage/deal-damage callback that switches `Pr` special-N states into hit behavior, adjusts velocity, resets callbacks, rotates the top joint, and plays SFX according to the diff excerpt.

The bot report recorded 42 new matches, including high-byte wins such as `ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Coll`, `ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim`, `ftKb_PrSpecialN1_Anim`, `ftKb_PrSpecialN1_Coll`, `ftKb_PrSpecialNTurn_Phys`, `ftKb_PrSpecialNTurn_Anim`, and `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNEndR_Anim`.
The bot also reported `.sdata2` improvement in the same unit, from 42.50% to 75.93%, consistent with added float constants and static motion-flag data.
smart_moves: Recognized that three adjacent fighter-var floats were actually a vector scale, enabling cleaner and matching-sensitive `Vec3` usage for JObj scale storage/restoration.

Used file-local fake `HSD_JObj` helper inlines with exact assert behavior and JObj dirty-matrix flag checks instead of calling generic helpers directly, likely to preserve original inlining/codegen patterns.
Factored repeated code into static inline helpers while still matching, e.g. shared Ness flash-item creation between ground and air start animations and shared `Pr` callback/scale/angle helpers.
Reduced duplicated air-physics implementations by making hold/end variants call the start physics body with small `PAD_STACK` adjustments, preserving stack/regalloc needs visible in the diff.
Promoted `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C` to accept a `bool` mode argument, allowing the same roll-SFX/angle-wrap helper to serve both normal and turn-related callers.
Kept unknown or address-derived names where intent was not fully recovered, such as `fn_80100E0C` and `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C`, avoiding premature semantic renaming.
decomp_lessons: Adjacent anonymous floats in fighter vars may be latent vector fields; verify by usage patterns such as `JObj` scale get/set before locking in scalar names.
For matching HSD/JObj-heavy code, reproducing assert strings, line numbers, flag checks, and dirty-matrix behavior in local inline wrappers can be more important than using cleaner library calls.
When several action callbacks share near-identical setup/teardown, try small static inline helpers first; this PR shows helpers can improve readability without sacrificing matches.
Prototype fixes can be required by matching work: here the newly decompiled implementation proved `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C` takes a boolean second parameter, and headers/docs had to follow.
Preserve stack padding explicitly when needed. The diff uses `PAD_STACK(20)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, and `PAD_STACK(4)` around otherwise straightforward code paths.

Copied-special code crosses several subsystems: fighter state, item spawning, effects pause/resume callbacks, collision hit capsules, stage/cliff collision, and JObj transforms. Matching these routines often requires adding the exact includes rather than relying on placeholders.
naming_conventions: File and symbol prefix `ftKb_` identifies Kirby-specific fighter code.

Copied neutral-special variants appear encoded in the symbol middle: `Ns` for Ness copied neutral special, `Dk` for Donkey Kong copied neutral special, and `Pr` for the `Pr` special-N family as shown by existing symbol names.
Action callback suffixes follow the fighter convention: `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Address-based names were retained for uncertain helpers and non-semantic routines, e.g. `ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FF8EC`, `ftKb_SpecialNPr_80100F94`, `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C`, and `fn_80100E0C`.

Shared helper names introduced in the diff are descriptive but scoped `static inline`, e.g. `ftKb_PrScaleAnimStep`, `ftKb_PrRestoreScale`, `ftKb_PrSetCallbacks`, and `ftKb_NsSpecialNStart_Anim_inline`.
Anonymous fighter-var fields remain `xNN`-style when not semantically identified, but the aggregate type can still be improved, as with `Vec3 x8C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` to tune stack layout/regalloc in multiple matched functions.
Used `static u32 const mf` bitmask locals for `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` flags in `Pr` state transitions.

Introduced static inline fake JObj accessors that copy `HSD_JObj` assert behavior and matrix-dirty checks: `ftKb_JObjGetScale`, `ftKb_JObjMtxIsDirty`, `ftKb_JObjSetScale`, and `ftKb_JObjSetRotationY`.
Normalized roll angles by looping within `0` to `M_PI * 2`, then applied rotations with `ftPartSetRotX`, `ftPartSetRotY`, and `M_PI_2`.
Used frame-indexed scale tables `ftKb_Init_803CB710` and `ftKb_Init_803CB720` to reproduce per-frame scale animation in `Pr` routines.

Matched item/effect lifecycle details: Ness flash item creation via `it_802AA8C0`, ownership checks via `it_802AA7E4`/`it_802AA7F0`, and effect pause/resume callbacks via `efLib_PauseAll`/`efLib_ResumeAll`.
Computed hit damage from current ground or air velocity and dat-attr multipliers in `ftKb_SpecialNPr_80100F94`, including disabling/enabling the hit capsule around a velocity threshold.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only comment was the automated decomp.dev report for GALE01, reporting matched code increased to 60.38% (+0.50%, +19,284 bytes), 42 new matches, and one `.sdata2` improvement.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `/// #` placeholders in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` after this PR.
Audit other Kirby copied-special fighter vars for adjacent floats that should be `Vec3` or another aggregate type.
Look for other local fake HSD/JObj helper patterns that can be reused or centralized only if doing so does not break matching.
Review callers of `ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C` to see whether `arg1` can eventually receive a semantic name once the `Pr` roll/turn behavior is better understood.
Compare `Pr` special-N callbacks with the source fighter's corresponding neutral-special implementation to improve names for `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x14`, and `x34` fields.
